name: Tiger
summary: Tigers are the largest wild cats in the world. They live alone in Asian forests and grasslands, hunt large prey at night, and are famous for their orange...
metaTitle: Tiger Facts | Habitat, Diet, Stripes & Photos
metaDescription: Discover tiger facts with photos, Asian habitat, hunting diet, cub life, and conservation. Learn where tigers live, what they eat, and why stripes help them...
speed: Up to 40 mph in short bursts
funFacts: Every tiger has a unique stripe pattern—like a fingerprint.
Tigers are strong swimmers and often rest in rivers to cool off.
A tiger's roar can be heard up to 2 miles (3 km) away.
Tigers usually hunt alone at night, using stealth rather than long chases.
A tiger can eat up to 88 pounds (40 kg) of meat in one meal after a big hunt.
Tiger cubs are born with blue eyes and stay with their mother for about two years.

faq.question: What is a tiger?
faq.answer: A tiger is a large wild cat and the biggest member of the cat family. Tigers live in Asia and hunt animals such as deer and wild pigs.

faq.question: Where do tigers live?
faq.answer: Wild tigers live in forests and grasslands across Asia, including India, Nepal, Russia, and Southeast Asia. Most live in protected reserves.

faq.question: What do tigers eat?
faq.answer: Tigers are carnivores that eat meat. They hunt deer, wild pigs, buffalo, and other large animals.

faq.question: Are tigers endangered?
faq.answer: Yes. Tigers are listed as Endangered because their numbers dropped sharply due to habitat loss and poaching.

faq.question: Do tigers live in groups?
faq.answer: Adult tigers usually live alone. Mothers raise cubs until the young tigers can hunt on their own.

faq.question: Why do tigers have stripes?
faq.answer: Stripes break up a tiger's outline in tall grass and forest shadows, helping it hide while hunting.
